what_it_does: Git ignore file listing paths excluded from version control: virtualenvs, build caches, node_modules, runtime store/data, telemetry logs, several secret credential/cert files, Obsidian per-user state, and the-one-system scratch dirs.
observations: Heavy emphasis on secrets: .secrets, .boundary.env, *.boundary.env, .cd-drive/.cd-history scripts+env, Tailscale TLS *.ts.net.key/.crt with explicit named host workstation001.tail777bc2.ts.net.*
Obsidian section: tracks shared settings but ignores workspace.json, workspace-mobile.json, cache, graph.json
Ignores build-prep/the-one-system/discovery/ and fusion-scope/ as regenerable scratch
Comments explain rationale inline (e.g. live EC private key managed on the box, not git)
standouts: Explicit named secret host workstation001.tail777bc2.ts.net.key/.crt called out as a live EC private key kept out-of-tree
Comments narrate WHY each secret class is ignored, not just what
conventions: Follows Obsidian-vault convention of tracking shared .obsidian settings while ignoring per-user workspace/cache
Annotates ignore groups with explanatory comments (secrets, runtime, scratch)
Distinguishes code from runtime-written data explicitly ('written by the live system, never code')
concerns: Hardcoding a specific Tailscale hostname in .gitignore couples the ignore rules to one machine; the generic *.ts.net.key/.crt globs already cover it
notes: If briefing someone: this gitignore doubles as a security boundary map — it enumerates where the repo's secrets and runtime data live out-of-tree.
questions: Are the named credential files (.boundary.env, *.ts.net.key) generated/rotated by a documented process elsewhere?
purpose_vs_actual: n/a — standard gitignore; content matches its evident purpose
intention_signals: Tries to keep all secrets and live runtime artifacts out of version control while keeping shared config tracked, treating the gitignore as documented security/boundary hygiene.
novelty: Inline comments turning a gitignore into a narrated map of the system's secrets and runtime boundaries.
contribution: Contributes the project's exclusion boundary: which venvs, runtime stores, telemetry, secrets/certs, Obsidian state, and scratch dirs stay out of git.
summary_for_embedding: Git ignore file for the company repo excluding venvs, runtime store/data, telemetry logs, named secret credentials and TLS certs, per-user Obsidian state, and the-one-system scratch directories.
intention_for_embedding: To keep secrets, live runtime data, and regenerable scratch out of version control while tracking shared configuration.
